vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high avionics heat load.
risk.mit: Implement enhanced cooling channels and thermal management systems.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure under extreme loading conditions.
risk.mit: Conduct thorough finite element analysis and reinforce critical areas.

risk.desc: Difficulty in maintaining precise alignment during assembly.
risk.mit: Use precision manufacturing techniques and alignment aids.

risk.desc: Complexity in service access and component replacement.
risk.mit: Optimize service access doors and modular design for easier maintenance.

risk.desc: Potential for electrical interference between avionics components.
risk.mit: Implement shielding and cabling standards to minimize interference.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.path: Wing spar load -> fuselage spine -> landing gear mount
Avionics cooling airflow -> avionics bay -> fuselage exterior
Heat from avionics -> fuselage spine -> radiated to atmosphere
Nacelle tilt mechanism -> bearing seat -> fuselage spine
Belly camera window -> camera housing -> external pressure
Avionics bay wiring -> harness passthrough -> wing spar socket
Service access door -> avionics bay -> maintenance

integration_opportunities.description: Add fuel tank mounting points to the fuselage spine
Install additional sensor mounts within the avionics bay
Enhance cooling channel design for improved heat dissipation

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: ±0.1mm for critical interfaces, ±0.2mm for non-critical features
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures removed post-CNC, orientation optimized for minimal warping
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit, CMM for dimensional accuracy

verification_focus.description: Verify load distribution and stress analysis
Ensure proper cooling airflow and heat dissipation
Check wiring harness routing and connector mating
Evaluate ease of maintenance and component replacement
Confirm mass estimation within design constraints

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient clearance for servo linkage during extreme tilt angles.
